column1: SCS-C02,"Domain 2: Security Logging & Monitoring","Security Logging & Monitoring","## SCS-C02 Domain 2: Security Logging & Monitoring
This domain covers designing centralized logging architectures, monitoring security events, and ensuring log integrity.

column3: Capture all accounts in org to single S3 bucket

column1: - SHA-256 hash of each log file
- Digest files are signed (public key from AWS)
- Detects: Deletion, modification, insertion of log files
-- Find all root account API calls
version account-id interface-id srcaddr dstaddr srcport dstport protocol packets bytes start end action log-status
### What Flow Logs Do NOT Capture
+-- CloudTrail (Organization Trail) --> Log Archive S3 Bucket
+-- VPC Flow Logs --> Log Archive S3 Bucket
Metric Filter (log pattern) -> CloudWatch Metric -> Alarm -> SNS -> Lambda/PagerDuty
- Use Composite Alarms to reduce noise
- Delivered to target S3 bucket (same region)
- Use for: Audit, access patterns, security analysis
- Detailed request information (client IP, latency, status)
- Use for: Troubleshooting, security analysis, compliance
- Use for: Audit trail of resource changes
- Log all DNS queries to Route 53 Resolver
- Contains: Domain queried, source IP, response
- Use for: Detect DNS exfiltration, malware C2 communication
- **""Audit all API calls across organization""** -> Organization Trail
- **""Detect log tampering""** -> Log file integrity validation
- **""Query CloudTrail logs""** -> Athena (S3) or CloudWatch Logs Insights
- **""Real-time alerting on API calls""** -> CloudTrail -> CloudWatch Logs -> Metric Filter -> Alarm
- **""S3 object-level logging""** -> CloudTrail Data Events (not S3 access logs)
- **""See rejected traffic""** -> VPC Flow Logs (REJECT action)
- **""Real-time flow analysis""** -> Flow Logs -> Kinesis Firehose -> OpenSearch
- **""Long-term flow storage""** -> Flow Logs -> S3 -> Athena
- **""Original source IP behind NAT""** -> Custom format with pkt-srcaddr
- **""Immutable log storage""** -> S3 Object Lock (Compliance mode)
- **""Prevent member accounts from disabling logging""** -> SCP
- **""Centralize logs from all accounts""** -> Organization Trail + dedicated Log Archive account
- **""Alert on root account usage""** -> CloudWatch Metric Filter + Alarm
- **""DNS-based data exfiltration""** -> Route 53 DNS Query Logging + GuardDuty
- **Organization Trail** captures ALL accounts. Individual trails are per-account.
- **Log file integrity** uses SHA-256 digest chain. Validate with CLI command.
- **S3 Object Lock Compliance mode** = nobody can delete, not even root. Use for log protection.
- **CloudTrail Data Events** are NOT enabled by default (extra cost). Needed for S3/Lambda/DynamoDB.
- **CloudTrail Insights** detects unusual API call patterns (spikes, new APIs).
- **VPC Flow Logs** capture metadata only (not packet content). Use Traffic Mirroring for packets.
- **Flow Logs cannot be modified** after creation. Delete and recreate to change format.
- **Metric Filters** extract metrics from CloudWatch Logs (e.g., count root logins).
- **Athena** is the cheapest way to query CloudTrail/Flow Logs stored in S3.
- **OpenSearch** (Kibana) for real-time dashboards and search across security logs.
- **SCPs** prevent member accounts from disabling CloudTrail or deleting logs.
- **Separate Log Archive account** isolates logs from workload account compromise.
- **DNS Query Logging** helps detect data exfiltration via DNS tunneling.
- **Config** records resource configuration changes (who changed what, when)."
